title: Tracker-neutral build plan
description: A tracker-agnostic plan of dependency-ordered IMPLEMENTATION tickets, each test-first: it carries the unit/integration tests to write BEFORE the code as inline TDD definition-of-done. Produced by the build-plan-architect agent from a technical spec (+ constraints.yaml + product spec); consumed by an execution agent and by tracker publishers. Holds INTENT only — no tracker-specific fields. Sibling to ticket-plan.schema.json (what to test) and acceptance-plan.schema.json (what journeys must pass); this one is what to BUILD, test-first.

version.description: Schema version. Bump only on breaking changes.

spec.description: Filename of the companion human-readable build plan (e.g. BUILD_PLAN.md).

constraints.description: Filename of the constraint envelope this plan honors (e.g. constraints.yaml). Referenced by tickets' constraintRefs; the validator checks those keys exist in it.

milestone.description: Single top-level grouping for all tickets. Adapters map this to a Linear Project/Milestone, a Jira Epic, or a GitHub Milestone. For one-sub-plan-per-epic fan-out, each component's sub-plan has its own milestone, merged on emit.

key.description: Stable, unique, kebab-case identifier. Used for idempotent re-publish.

tickets.description: Flat list of implementation tickets. Hierarchy/ordering via parent + blockedBy by key.

ticketKey.description: Stable, unique, kebab-case ticket identity. Referenced by parent/blockedBy and STAMPED onto the created issue for idempotent re-publish.

prId.description: A product-requirement ID from the product spec (the traceability spine).

title.description: Concise human title. Prefix with the layer for scannability, e.g. 'Feature: guest order placement'.

layer.description: What kind of build work this is. feature/interface/migration are code-bearing and MUST carry tddCases (test-first); the rest support them.

stack.description: Stack/area key for polyglot repos (e.g. 'python', 'js-frontend', 'js-node'). Omit or use '-' for single-stack.

priority.description: Neutral priority. Adapters map to the tracker's scale via per-team config.

tier.description: Optional, advisory model-routing hint for an orchestrator. Tier prices residual design ambiguity, not domain riskiness: 'simple' = mechanical, well-bounded, a cheap model can drive red->green->refactor unattended; 'standard' = normal feature work, the default -- including security/migration work whose interface+tddCases fully pin the design; 'complex' = genuine design judgment remains at build time, reserve a stronger model and/or human review (budget ~1 in 10; the validator caps the share at 15%). The plan stays declarative; the orchestrator decides what each tier maps to via per-team config.

labels.description: Free-form neutral labels. Adapters may prefix/translate them -- EXCEPT the orchestration hints, which pass through verbatim: mod:<area> (component seam; execution orchestrators parallelize by it) and resource:<name> (shared mutable resource needing exclusive use, e.g. resource:db on migrations; orchestrators never run two tickets sharing one in parallel).

description.description: Markdown body: why this ticket exists and what it builds. Adapters render this plus the other fields into the issue body.

interface.description: The contract to implement — function/class/endpoint signatures, request/response shapes — taken from the technical spec's boundaries. The seam, not full code. Strongly expected on feature/interface tickets.

tddCases.description: The unit/integration cases to write FIRST (red), as 'input -> expected', including edges and the failure modes from unit-integration-standards.md. Required for code-bearing layers (feature/interface/migration). These are the failing tests that specify the work.

modulesInScope.description: REAL, verified file paths an implementer will touch or read: the module(s) to create/edit, the test file to create (with the correct naming infix), and shared fixtures/harness/types/config. Never placeholders.

constraintRefs.description: Keys from constraints.yaml this ticket must honor (e.g. 'lang-python', 'db-postgres'). The validator checks each key exists in the referenced constraints file, so compliance is mechanically checkable.

tracesTo.description: PR-* product-requirement IDs this ticket helps satisfy (the traceability spine). The validator checks each exists in the product spec.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Checkable Definition of Done as a checklist — not a restatement of the cases. For code-bearing layers this MUST encode the inner loop: tddCases written first & failing, then passing, refactored clean, coverage floor met, lint/type-check green, no skipped tests, constraints honored.

parent.description: Optional key of a parent ticket. A RELATIONSHIP, not nesting; adapters render as sub-issue/sub-task/checklist. Must reference another ticket's key.

blockedBy.description: Keys of tickets that must be done first (a prerequisite interface, migration, refactor, or harness). Adapters map to 'blocked by' relations/links. Each must reference another ticket's key.

allOf.description: Code-bearing layers must enumerate at least one tddCase (test-first).
